name: colorful-test
documentation: # Colorful Test

classes.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.set_up
documentation:
This method is called immediately before calling a test method;
other than AssertionError or SkipTest, any exception raised by
this method will be considered an error rather than a test
failure. The default implementation does nothing.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.tear_down
documentation:
This method is called immediately after calling a test method;
other than AssertionError or SkipTest, any exception raised by
this method will be considered an error rather than a test
failure. This method will be executed only if set_up succeeds.
The default implemantation does nothing.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.set_up_class
documentation:
This method is called before tests in an individual class run. The
default implementation does nothing.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.tear_down_class
documentation:
This method is called after tests in an individual class run. The
default implementation does nothing.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.run
documentation:
Run the tests, collecting the results into TestResult object. The
result object is returned to run()'s caller.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.skip_test
documentation: Calling this during a test method or setUp() skips the current test.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_equal
documentation:
Test that first and second are equal. If the values does not 
compare, the test will fail.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_not_equal
documentation:
Test that first and second are not equal. If the values do compare
equal, the test will fail

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_true
documentation: Test that expr is True.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_false
documentation: Test that expr is False.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_is
documentation: Test that first and second evaluate to the same object.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_is_not
documentation: Test that first and second does not evaluate to the same object.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_is_none
documentation: Test that expr is None.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_is_not_none
documentation: Test that expr is not None.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_in
documentation: Test that first is in second.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_not_in
documentation: Test that first is not in second.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_is_instance
documentation: Test that obj is an instance of cls.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_not_is_instance
documentation: Test that obj is not an instance of cls.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_raises
documentation:
Test that an exception (specific) is raised when callable is
called with any positional or keyword arguments. The test passes
if exception is raised, is an error if another exception is 
raised, or fails if no exception is raised.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_does_not_raises
documentation:
Test that an exception (specific) is not raised when callable is
called with any positional or keyword arguments. The test passes
if exception is not raised, is an error if another exception is 
raised, or fails if exception is raised.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_raises_regex
documentation:
Like assertRaises() but also tests that regex matches on the 
string representation of the raised exception.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_almost_equal
documentation:
Test that first and second are approximately equal by computing
the difference, rounding to the given number of decimal places
(default 7), and comparing to zero.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_not_almost_equal
documentation:
Test that first and second are not approximately equal by
computing the difference, rounding to the given number of decimal
places (default 7), and comparing to zero.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_greater
documentation: Test that first is > than the second. If not, the test will fail.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_greater_equal
documentation: Test that first is >= than the second. If not, the test will fail.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_less
documentation: Test that first is < than the second. If not, the test will fail.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_less_equal
documentation: Test that first is <= than the second. If not, the test will fail.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_regex
documentation: Test that a regex search matches the text.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_not_regex
documentation: Test that a regex search does not math the text.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_count_equal
documentation:
Test that sequence first contains the same elements as second,
regardless of their order. Duplicates are not ignored.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_sequence_equal
documentation:
Test that two sequences are equal. If a seq_type is supplied, 
both first and second must be instances of seq_type or a failure
will be raised.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_list_equal
documentation: Test that two lists are equal.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_tuple_equal
documentation: Test that two tuples are equal.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_set_equal
documentation:
Test that two sets are equal.

Fails if either of first or second does not have a 
set.difference() method.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.assert_dict_equal
documentation: Test that two dictionaries are equal.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.add_cleanup
documentation:
Add a function to be called after tearDown() to clean up resources
used during the test. Functions will be called in reverse order to
the order they are added (LIFO). They are called with any 
arguments and keyword arguments passed into addCleanup when they 
are added.

If setUp() fails meaning that tearDown() is not called, then any 
cleanup functions will still be called.

methods.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.do_cleanups
documentation:
The method is called unconditionally after tearDown(), or after 
setUp() if setUp() raises an exception.

inner_classes.name: colorful_test.testcase.TestCase.TestResult
documentation:
Class that stores the results of the tests ran by run(). It provides
functionalities to display output to the screen.

Base class that implements the interface needed by the runner to allow
it do drive the tests, and methods that the test code can use to check 
for and report various kinds of failures.
